os: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.3 and earlier, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6 with Red Hat MRG v2.0 real time kernel, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6
scenario: System hangs after (Broadcom NICs) "bnx2x_sp_post: BUG! EQ ring"
symptoms: PANIC: "Oops: System Reset, sig: 6 [#1]" (check log for details)
kernel: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#12 stuck for 22s! [migration/12:391]
kernel: INFO: rcu_sched detected stalls on CPUs/tasks: { 79 107 121 128 131 137 138 139 140 156 158} (detected by 104, t=240067 jiffies, g=77851189, c=77851188, q=0)

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of oldest failed task to understand semaphore acquisition failure.
workflow.observation: Task stuck in rwsem_down_write_failed waiting for write lock.

workflow.thought: Inspect mmap_sem structure to see semaphore state and waiters.
workflow.observation: Semaphore count shows waiters; wait_list contains pending tasks.

workflow.thought: List all tasks waiting on the semaphore to identify contention.
workflow.observation: Many tasks waiting for read lock; one task waiting for write lock.

workflow.thought: Check backtrace of another waiting task to see what holds the semaphore.
workflow.observation: Task in migrate_swap trying to stop CPUs while holding mmap_sem.

workflow.thought: Examine migration_swap_arg structure to understand CPU stop targets.
workflow.observation: Migration swap involves tasks on CPUs 12 and 79.

workflow.thought: Inspect backtrace of the destination task (PID 10250) on CPU 79.
workflow.observation: Task in system reset exception; crash utility confused by endianness.

workflow.thought: Examine kthread_worker structure to check spinlock state.
workflow.observation: Spinlock slock value 0x8000009e indicates locked state.

workflow.thought: Convert hex lock value to decimal to identify locking CPU.
Check backtrace of task on CPU 158 to see why it holds the lock.
workflow.observation: Task in wake_up_process spinning because target task is on_cpu.

workflow.thought: Disassemble wake_up_process to understand spinning loop.
workflow.observation: Code shows while(p->on_cpu) cpu_relax() loop at line 1711.

workflow.thought: Check task_struct fields for on_cpu and on_rq status of PID 10250.
workflow.observation: Task has on_cpu=1 and on_rq=1, indicating it's currently running.

root_cause_analysis: The system has found a race condition which can lead to a deadlock condition in the scheduler when one task tries to wake up an already running task.
This race condition has resulted in one task spinning in a loop while waiting for a second task to stop running.
But the second task cannot stop running as it is spinning waiting on a spinlock owned by the first task.
This then resulted in NIC firmware hang.
